Description: Improve an existing plan by getting an agent to expand on the details and implementation ideas.

content: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. If the file contains only the legacy shape `{ "plan_path": "..." }`, treat that as an empty `additional_repositories` list. The current repository is the canonical plan host and is implicitly in scope. If it also appears inside `additional_repositories`, treat that as redundant and ignore that entry. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository, that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename, and that every additional repository is either already on that same story branch or can safely create or reuse that branch without overwriting local changes. If those do not match, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Do not independently search for or select a different plan. Read the plan's `Additional Repositories` section, supporting both `## Additional Repositories` and `### Additional Repositories`, and ensure it stays in sync with the repository names used by the tasks. Do not silently introduce work in a new repository unless you first add that repository to the plan's `Additional Repositories` section. Without tasking up, is there any acceptance in this upcoming story plan that is not defined in a way that a junior developer could understand what the output should be? If so, please update all relevant planning sections to get them to where we need to be. Do not provide options, just make the best changes you can based on what you know and what I just asked. Commit after making any changes.

Without tasking up, do you feel this story is well scoped? How could it be improved? Are there unknowns that need to be researched? If so, please research them using the codeInfo, DeepWiki, and Context7 MCP tools, as well as cross-referencing with internet searches, to gather repository facts, likely file locations, summaries of existing implementations, current contracts, and other evidence. Then inspect the relevant source files directly and do your own reasoning before updating the plan with your findings. Remember to give the repository path to the code_info tool as part of your question so it uses the correct repository. Do not provide options, just make the best changes you can based on what you found while researching. Commit after making any changes.

Without actually tasking up, can you create a rough plan, or update an existing one if it is there, on what will need to be changed in order to implement this story? Please use the code_info, DeepWiki, and Context7 MCP tools to gather repository facts, existing implementations, current contracts, and candidate files, then inspect the relevant source files directly and do your own reasoning before writing the plan. Place the information into a section named Implementation Ideas within the plan. Remember to give the repository path to the code_info tool as part of your question so it uses the correct repository. Do not provide options, just make the best changes you can based on what you found while researching. Commit after making any changes.

Without tasking up, does the story require any new message contracts or storage shapes, or changes to existing ones? If so, can these be defined within their own section of the story up front based on what we know is needed, and if there are already definitions in the plan, then please update them? Please use the code_info, DeepWiki, and Context7 MCP tools to gather existing contracts, storage shapes, and candidate files, then inspect the relevant source files directly and do your own reasoning so we do not duplicate anything that already exists. Remember to give the repository path to the code_info tool as part of your question so it uses the correct repository. Do not provide options, just make the best changes you can based on what you found while researching. Commit after making any changes.

Without tasking up, are there any sections in this story that are not defined in a way that a junior developer could understand what the output should be, and what the story needs to achieve? If so, please update all relevant planning sections to get them to where we need to be. Do not provide options, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Without tasking up, is everything in the planning consistent, or are there some contradictions, especially across the various sections? If so, please resolve and remove the contradictions based on what we know we actually want. Do not provide options, just make the best changes you feel would be best. If you are unsure, use git tools to compare the conflicting requirements and ensure we normalise on the latest commit. Commit after making any changes.

Without actually tasking up, do you feel this story is detailed enough to be tasked up by someone? If not, please make the required changes to the plan. Do not provide options, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Without tasking up, are there any edge cases or failure modes that need to be considered for this story? If so, please add them to the plan in their own section named Edge Cases and Failure Modes. Please use the code_info, DeepWiki, and Context7 MCP tools to gather existing implementations, current contracts, and candidate files, then inspect the relevant source files directly and do your own reasoning about which edge cases and failure modes need to be covered. Remember to give the repository path to the code_info tool as part of your question so it uses the correct repository. Do not provide options, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.

Please create tasks for this story, ensuring that each task only implements one thing that can be tested. Server changes to messages should be tasked separately and prior to the front-end changes that rely on them unless changing the server will break the front end. Ensure that each task is detailed enough for a junior developer to work through without the input of a senior. Base the formatting, detail, and structure of the tasks on previous story tasks. Do not provide options, just make the best changes you feel would be best. Commit after making any changes.
